title: Get started with chatbotr
text: simplest way add chat bubble using floating_chat_ui() floating_chat_server(). must provide ellmer chat client.
code: library(shiny) library(bslib) library(chatbotr)  ui <- page_fluid(   floating_chat_ui("chat") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   # Requires GITHUB_PAT if using chat_github()   chat <- ellmer::chat_github()   floating_chat_server("chat", client = chat) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Get started with chatbotr
text: prefer slide-panel, use offcanvas_* variants:
code: ui <- page_fluid(   offcanvas_chat_ui("chat") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   floating_chat_server("chat", client = ellmer::chat_github()) }

what: Let users bring their own key
title: Get started with chatbotr
text: common pattern let users configure LLM client runtime. chatbotr provides api_settings_ui() api_settings_server() purpose.
code: ui <- page_fluid(   api_settings_ui("settings"),   floating_chat_ui("chat") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   settings <- api_settings_server("settings")      observe({     req(settings$is_configured())     floating_chat_server("chat", client = settings$client())   }) }

title: Get started with chatbotr
text: Ensure using ellmer (>= 0.4.0.9000).

what: Shiny Chatbot Application with Offcanvas Interface
title: Shiny Chatbot Application with Offcanvas Interface
text: floating chatbot Shiny. every ecosystem one R didn’t. Python chat widgets. JavaScript chat widgets. R/Shiny textInput() prayer. chatbotr adds floating chat bubble (slide-panel) top {shinychat} Shiny app, backed LLM via {ellmer}. Bring key.

title: Shiny Chatbot Application with Offcanvas Interface
text: [!IMPORTANT] Requires ellmer >= 0.4.0.9000, use GitHub API model provider: unreleased PR pak also resolves shinychat remote (posit-dev/shinychat) don’t think .

title: Shiny Chatbot Application with Offcanvas Interface
text: Chat bubble, bottom-right corner. Click, talk, close. Dashboard unharmed.
code: library(shiny); library(bslib); library(chatbotr)  ui <- page_fluid(   floating_chat_ui("chat") ) server <- function(input, output, session) {   floating_chat_server("chat", client = ellmer::chat_github(model = "gpt-5-mini")) } shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Shiny Chatbot Application with Offcanvas Interface
text: accept ellmer client. GitHub Copilot, OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini — dealer’s choice.

title: Shiny Chatbot Application with Offcanvas Interface
text: Let users supply API key runtime: set OPENAI_API_KEY .Renviron move life.
code: ui <- page_fluid(   api_settings_ui("settings"),   floating_chat_ui("chat") ) server <- function(input, output, session) {   settings <- api_settings_server("settings")   observe({     req(settings$is_configured())     floating_chat_server("chat", client = settings$client())   }) } shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Shiny Chatbot Application with Offcanvas Interface
text: MIT

title: API Settings Server Module — api_settings_server
text: Server logic API settings configuration. Handles provider selection, API key validation, model selection, client creation.

title: API Settings Server Module — api_settings_server
text: id Namespace ID matching UI module default_system_prompt Default system prompt LLM (optional)

title: API Settings Server Module — api_settings_server
text: reactive list containing: client: Configured ellmer client (reactive) provider: Selected provider (reactive) model: Selected model (reactive) is_configured: Whether settings valid (reactive)

title: API Settings Server Module — api_settings_server
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ server <- function(input, output, session) {   settings <- api_settings_server("settings")    # Use the configured client   observe({     if (settings$is_configured()) {       client <- settings$client()       # Use client with shinychat     }   }) } } # }

title: API Settings UI Module — api_settings_ui
text: Creates UI configuring API keys selecting LLM providers models. Supports multiple providers (GitHub Models, OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, etc.) secure key input model selection.

title: API Settings UI Module — api_settings_ui
code: api_settings_ui(   id,   default_provider = "github",   show_advanced = FALSE,   inline = FALSE )

title: API Settings UI Module — api_settings_ui
text: id Namespace ID module default_provider Default provider show (default: "github") show_advanced Show advanced settings like temperature, max_tokens (default: FALSE) inline Display settings inline vs modal (default: FALSE)

title: API Settings UI Module — api_settings_ui
text: Shiny UI element containing settings interface

text: Creates JS code handle clicking prompt chips.

text: container_id DOM ID container element

title: Generate JavaScript for suggested prompt clicks — chat_prompts_js
text: shiny tags$script element

title: Generate suggested prompts UI — chat_prompts_ui
text: Creates horizontal scrolling chip container suggested prompts.

title: Generate suggested prompts UI — chat_prompts_ui
text: prompts Character vector prompt strings border_color CSS color top border (default: "#e5e5e5") bg_color CSS color background (default: "#fff")

title: Generate suggested prompts UI — chat_prompts_ui
text: shiny tags$div NULL prompts

what: Shared CSS for chat components — chat_shared_css
title: Shared CSS for chat components — chat_shared_css
text: Returns CSS styles shared floating offcanvas chat modules.

Shared CSS for chat components — chat_shared_css
text: include_dark_theme Whether include dark theme variants (default: FALSE)

title: Shared CSS for chat components — chat_shared_css
text: shiny tags$style element

what: Detect API key from environment variables — detect_env_key
title: Detect API key from environment variables — detect_env_key
text: Checks provider's supported environment variables set returns information first one found.

title: Detect API key from environment variables — detect_env_key
Detect API key from environment variables — detect_env_key
text: provider Provider ID (e.g., "github", "openai", "anthropic")

title: Detect API key from environment variables — detect_env_key
text: list var_name, key, masked (preview), NULL environment variable set

title: Floating Chat Server Module — floating_chat_server
text: Server logic floating chat module. function wraps another chat server module (like shinychat's server) within floating chat namespace.

title: Floating Chat Server Module — floating_chat_server
text: id Namespace ID matching UI module client Chat client object (ellmer) ... Additional arguments passed chat_server_fun

title: Floating Chat Server Module — floating_chat_server
text: Returns result chat server function

title: Floating Chat UI Module — floating_chat_ui
text: Creates floating chat interface customizable trigger icon collapsible overlay panel. chat appears modern floating window top main content, similar common chatbot interfaces.

title: Floating Chat UI Module — floating_chat_ui
code: floating_chat_ui(   id,   title = "Chat Assistant",   trigger_position = c("bottom-right", "bottom-left", "top-right", "top-left"),   trigger_icon = "comments",   trigger_size = 60,   panel_width = 400,   panel_height = 600,   panel_offset = 20,   chat_ui_args = list(),   welcome_message = NULL,   suggested_prompts = NULL,   theme = c("light", "dark"),   enable_minimize = TRUE,   enable_maximize = TRUE,   header_actions = NULL )

title: Floating Chat UI Module — floating_chat_ui
text: id Namespace ID module title Title displayed chat panel header (default: "Chat Assistant") trigger_position Position floating trigger icon: "bottom-right", "bottom-left", "top-right", "top-left" (default: "bottom-right") trigger_icon Icon name trigger button (default: "comments") trigger_size Size trigger button pixels (default: 60) panel_width Width chat panel pixels (default: 400) panel_height Height chat panel pixels (default: 600) panel_offset Offset viewport edges pixels (default: 20) chat_ui_args Named list additional arguments passed shinychat::chat_mod_ui() welcome_message Optional welcome message display chat first opened. provided, passed chat UI function via chat_ui_args. suggested_prompts Optional vector suggested prompt strings display chips. theme Color theme: "light" "dark" (default: "light") enable_minimize Enable minimize functionality (default: TRUE) enable_maximize Enable maximize functionality (default: TRUE) header_actions Optional UI elements header actions (e.g., clear button)

title: Floating Chat UI Module — floating_chat_ui
text: Shiny UI tagList containing floating trigger chat panel

title: Floating Chat UI Module — floating_chat_ui
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(shiny) library(bslib)  ui <- page_fluid(   theme = bs_theme(version = 5),   h3("My App"),   floating_chat_ui(     id = "floating_chat",     title = "AI Assistant",     trigger_position = "bottom-right",     welcome_message = "Hello! I'm your AI assistant. How can I help you today?"   ) ) } # }

text: Converts numeric values pixel strings, passes string values.

title: Format CSS dimension — format_dimension
text: dim numeric value (pixels) string units (e.g., "30vw")

title: Format CSS dimension — format_dimension
text: CSS-compatible dimension string

what: Get environment variable names for a provider — get_provider_env_var
title: Get environment variable names for a provider — get_provider_env_var
text: Returns environment variable name(s) can used API key authentication given provider. providers support multiple environment variable names.

title: Get environment variable names for a provider — get_provider_env_var
Get environment variable names for a provider — get_provider_env_var
text: provider Provider ID (e.g., "github", "openai", "anthropic")

title: Get environment variable names for a provider — get_provider_env_var
text: Character vector environment variable names, NULL provider use API keys (e.g., ollama)

title: Null coalescing operator — %||%
text: Null coalescing operator

title: Null coalescing operator — %||%
text: x Value check y Default value x NULL

title: Null coalescing operator — %||%
text: x NULL, otherwise y

title: Offcanvas Chat Server Module — offcanvas_chat_server
text: Server logic offcanvas chat module. function wraps another chat server module (like shinychat's server) within offcanvas namespace.

title: Offcanvas Chat Server Module — offcanvas_chat_server
text: id Namespace ID matching UI module client Chat client object (ellmer) ... Additional arguments passed chat_mod_server

title: Offcanvas Chat Server Module — offcanvas_chat_server
text: Returns result chat server function

title: Offcanvas Chat UI Module — offcanvas_chat_ui
text: Creates offcanvas (slide-) panel containing chat interface. offcanvas can slide side screen.

title: Offcanvas Chat UI Module — offcanvas_chat_ui
code: offcanvas_chat_ui(   id,   title = "Chat",   placement = c("end", "start", "bottom", "top"),   width = 420,   open_label = "Chat",   open_class = "btn btn-dark",   open_icon = NULL,   chat_ui_args = list(),   welcome_message = NULL,   header_right = NULL,   suggested_prompts = NULL )

title: Offcanvas Chat UI Module — offcanvas_chat_ui
text: id Namespace ID module title Title displayed offcanvas header (default: "Chat") placement Position offcanvas: "end" (right), "start" (left), "bottom", "top" (default: "end") width Width pixels side placement (default: 420) open_label Text label button opens chat (default: "Chat") open_class CSS classes open button (default: "btn btn-dark") open_icon Optional icon name open button (e.g., "comments") chat_ui_args Named list additional arguments passed shinychat::chat_mod_ui welcome_message Optional welcome message display chat first opened. provided, passed chat UI function via chat_ui_args. header_right Optional UI elements display header's right side suggested_prompts Optional vector suggested prompt strings display chips.

title: Offcanvas Chat UI Module — offcanvas_chat_ui
text: Shiny UI tagList containing open button offcanvas panel

title: Offcanvas Chat UI Module — offcanvas_chat_ui
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(shiny) library(bslib)  ui <- page_fluid(   theme = bs_theme(version = 5),   h3("My App"),   offcanvas_chat_ui(     id = "chat",     title = "Assistant",     placement = "end",     welcome_message = "Welcome! How can I assist you?"   ) ) } # }

title: Shared Utilities for chatbotr — utils
text: Internal utility functions used across chat modules. exported internal use .
